title: Prompt Engineering
description: Test your knowledge of designing effective prompts for large language models

questions.question: What is 'few-shot prompting'?
options: Prompting the model as few times as possible to save cost
Including a small number of worked examples in the prompt to guide the model
Sending prompts in short bursts to avoid rate limits
Using the shortest possible wording for every instruction
questions.explanation: Few-shot prompting supplies a handful of input/output examples so the model can infer the pattern you want it to follow.

questions.question: Why does 'chain-of-thought' prompting often improve answers on reasoning tasks?
options: It encrypts the prompt so the model pays more attention
It asks the model to work through intermediate steps before answering
It chains several models together in sequence
It reduces the size of the context window
questions.explanation: Asking the model to reason step by step gives it room to break a problem into intermediate steps, which improves accuracy on multi-step reasoning.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of a 'system prompt'?
options: To store the conversation history on disk
To set the model's persistent role, tone and constraints for the conversation
To restart the model when it crashes
To translate the user's language automatically
questions.explanation: A system prompt establishes high-level instructions — role, tone, rules — that shape how the model responds throughout the conversation.

questions.question: Which technique most directly reduces ambiguity in a prompt?
options: Raising the temperature setting
Giving explicit, specific instructions and a desired output format
Making the prompt as short as possible
Repeating the same word many times
questions.explanation: Clear, specific instructions and an explicit output format leave the model less room to guess, producing more predictable results.

questions.question: What does raising the 'temperature' parameter typically do?
options: Makes responses more random and varied
Makes responses more deterministic and focused
Increases the context window size
Speeds up token generation
questions.explanation: Higher temperature increases randomness in token selection, yielding more diverse (and less predictable) output; lower temperature makes it more focused.
